gloss.en: round parts a vehicle turns on

words.example: The car has four wheels.
This is the problem wherein we disagree.

gloss.en: selling goods in large amounts cheaply

words.example: They buy goods at wholesale prices.
This road is wider than the old one.
My friend William lives nearby.
Mr. Williams teaches our class.
Dr. Wilson saw the patient today.
Please open the windows for fresh air.
The bird spread its wings and flew.
She had the winning number in the game.

gloss.en: a state in the United States

words.example: They grew up in Wisconsin.
She wished for a sunny day.
He sent his best wishes to us.
The offer was withdrawn last week.

gloss.en: people who saw an event happen

words.example: The witnesses told the police what they saw.
The two men brought their wives.
Many women work in this office.
She wondered where he had gone.
We walked through the woods.

gloss.en: units of language; what we say

words.example: Choose your words with care.

gloss.en: had clothes on; past of wear

words.example: He wore a blue coat yesterday.

gloss.en: did a job; past of work

words.example: She worked late last night.
The workers built the new bridge.
The workmen fixed the road today.
He works in a big factory.
The wounded soldier needed help.
Mr. Wright owns the shop.
The court issued a writ.

gloss.en: people who write books or articles

words.example: Many writers came to the meeting.
She writes a letter every week.
His writings are very famous.

gloss.en: put words on paper; past of write

words.example: He wrote a long letter home.

gloss.en: units of length; areas near a house

words.example: The garden is ten yards long.
She lived here for many years.
This field yields a good crop.
They visited York last summer.
My younger sister is ten.

gloss.en: part of the name New Zealand

words.example: New Zealand is a beautiful country.
This coin is made of zinc.
The city has several quiet zones.
